follows_up: opaque-markets (Run 12) — claims flagged at 55-148 TPS net-new

claim_events_administrative.notes: Submission + adjudication + status messages. Mostly EDI, not money movement. Dominated by US + global health (medical + pharmacy + dental).

settlement_payments_money_movement.notes: Actual payouts. Shrunk from Layer 1 by ACH remittance batching (4-8 claims per payment) and denials/zero-pay (~25%).

net_new_to_big_number.notes: Claim payments NOT already on counted bank/card rails: instant/push-to-card disbursements, RTP payouts, emerging-market cash/cross-border settlement. ~85-90% of settlement value already rides counted ACH/card rails.

us_health.anchor: CAQH 31.5B administrative verifications 2024 confirms order of magnitude

global_health.notes: US ~2.5x scale-up; single-payer systems adjudicate differently, so ratio < premium share

global_life_annuity.notes: Low count, high value per payout — value/count inversion in miniature

batch_ratio.notes: Dominant uncertainty. Health payers batch adjudicated claims into single ACH remittance (EFT/ERA). Small changes swing estimate +/- 40 TPS.

recommendation: Do NOT add as a standalone Big Number category. Treat as documented adjacency ~95 TPS net-new. Re-measure annually as push-to-card / RTP instant claim disbursement converts batched ACH (low count) to 1:1 instant (high count).

confidence.notes: Upgraded on CAQH (31.5B verifications) and NACHA healthcare-ACH anchors. Batch ratio (4-8x) remains the dominant uncertainty. Global (non-US) claim counts estimated. Life/annuity count is directional.
